answer:
The correct answer is **\boxed{A}**.

For **(a)**, the eigenvalues of \(\begin{bmatrix} 3 & 1 \\ 1 & 3 \end{bmatrix}\) are found by solving \(\det(A - \lambda I) = 0\), yielding \(\lambda = 4, 1\).

For **(b)**, the eigenvalues of the \(3 \times 3\) matrix \(\begin{bmatrix} 2 & 2 & 1 \\ 1 & 3 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & 2 \end{bmatrix}\) are \(3, 1, 1\).

For **(c)**, the eigenvalues of \(\begin{bmatrix} 2 & -1 & 0 \\ 9 & 4 & 6 \\ -8 & 0 & -3 \end{bmatrix}\) are \(1, -1, 4\).
